titleStmt.title: Letter to Dr Thomas Percy Kirkpatrick, 7 February 1917
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: From 1910 until 1954 Thomas Percy Kirkpatrick (1869-1954) served as the registrar for the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland. He also served as the general secretary of the Royal Academy of Medicine in Ireland. He took a particular interest in what were then termed venereal diseases; to encourage his patients (many of whom were prostitutes) to attend, he held a clinic for women at a discreet early morning hour to facilitate anonymity.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
National Council for Combating Venereal Diseases. President: The Right Hon. LORD SYDENHAM of Combe, G.C.S.I., etc,
Vice-Presidents:
Sir THOMAS BARLOW, BART., K,C,V,O M,D The LORD BISHOP OF SOUTHWARK.
MEMBERS OF THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE:
Sir Clifford Allbutt, K.C.B., M,D., F,R,S. Rev. J. Scott Lidgett, M.A., D.D
Sir Francis Champneys, BART., M.D Dr. C.J. Macalister.
Sir Watson Cheyne, BART., F.R.,S P Sir Malcolm Morris, K.C.V.Vo., F.R.
President Royal College of Surgeons. Dr. Mott, F.R.S.
Sir JOhn Collie, M.D Miss M.L. Rawlins, M.B
Mr. W.A.Coote. Mrs. Scharlieb, M.D
Mrs. Creighton. Mr. Charters Symonds, F.R.C.S.
Major Leonard Darwin (Hon. Treasurer). Dr. Frederick Taylor,
Colonel T.W. Gibbard, R.A.M.C President Royal College of Physicians
Sir Rickman Godlee, BART., K.C.V.O., F.R.C.S. Mrs Torrey.
Mrs. James Gow. Mr. E.B. Turner, F.R.C.S.
Dr. T. Wardrop Griffith. Mrs. R.P. Wethered.
Lord George Hamilton. Dr. Douglas White.
Mr. J. Ernest Lane, F.R.C.S
Hon. Secretaries— DR. OTTO MAY and MRS. A.C. GOTTO
Secretary— MISS H.F. NORRY. BANK BUILDINGS,
KINGSWAY, LONDON, W.C. 7th February, 1917 Dr. Kirkpatrick,
11, Fitzwilliam Place,
Dublin. Dear Sir, I have just heard from Sir John Moore that in all pro-
bability you would be interested in the work of this Council. It is hoped that a Dublin Committee of Council may
be formed in the near future, if you would be willing to
co-operate with those members of Council already in
Dublin who are proposing to take the initiative in the
formation of Committee, would you communicate with
Sir John Moore. I enclose further particulars of this organisation, and
need not say we should be most grateful for your support. Yours very truly J. Fetto
Joint Hon. Secretary.
